def: s.f. The being collected together (like a bud); budding, blooming.
s.m. (dialec.) A furrow.

r: gund, guṇḍ, guṇa-da, guṇad, guṇ, gūṅd, goṅd
def: s.m. corr. of guṇḍ, q.v.; — and of guṇa-da or guṇad, q.v.s.v. guṇ; — s.f. corr. of gūṅd; and goṅd, qq.v.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit गन्ध (gandha, “smell, stink”). Cognate with Persian گنده (gande, “stenchy”).

def: adj. = گنده gunda, q.v.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Dissolute, rakish; foppish; — a dissolute fellow, a rake; a fop; — an astute fellow; a sharper, knave, rogue, rascal.
s.m. A fop.
s.m. corr. of guṇḍā, q.v.
adj. Coarse, thick, large, gross.

ety: gunda, q.v.+ī = Zend i = S. इ
def: s.f. Thickness, coarseness, grossness, bigness.
s.m. = H گنڐلي गुण्डली guṇḍalī, s.f. See kuṇḍalā, and kuṇḍalī.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Round, circular; — a ring; circle; a coil.
s.f. = H گنڐلا गुण्डला guṇḍalā, s.m. See kuṇḍalā, and kuṇḍalī.
v.t. (dialec.) = gūṅdhnā, and gundhwānā.
adj. Gum-producing (a tree).
s.m. Dough ( = gūṅdhan).
s.m. Kneading; — dough (cf. goṅdā); — plaiting, braiding.
s.f. Plainting; braiding.

ety: neuter of gūṅdhnā, q.v.; and see also guthnā
def: v.n. To be kneaded; — to be plaited, &c.
v.t. To knead (dough); — to plait, braid; to plat, weave.
v.t. To cause to be kneaded, &c. (see gūṅdhnā).
v.t. prop. guṅdhwānā, q.v.
s.m. (dialec.) = ghunḍī, q.v
v.t. = gūṅdhnā, q.v.
s.m. A buzzing or humming sound (in the ear).
adj. Dumb, mute, speechless.
adj. = gūṅgā, q.v.

ety: prob. for gūṅ-gāṅ, 'inarticulate sound' = S. क्वण redupl.; — or onomat.
def: adj. (f. -ī), Dumb, mute, speechless
subs: s.f. A riddle asked in dumb-show (e.g. a finger placed on the forehead denotes karam, 'fate'; and the finger on the cheek signifies kallā, 'cheek': the two words together make karam-kallā, 'cabbage').

ety: gūṅgā, q.v.+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: s.f. Dumbness, speechlessness.

ety: gūṅgā, q.v.+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: s.f. Dumbness, speechlessness.
s.m. = guṇ-gān, q.v.s.v. guṇ.
adj. = guṅgī or gūṅgī, fem. of gūṅgā, q.v.; — s.f. = ghūṅgī; and ghoṅghī, qq.v.
s.f. Buzzing, humming; suppressed or inarticulate speaking or reading; speaking softly to oneself; muttering; grumbling; — snuffling, snuffle; a nasal sound or twang.
s.m. (f. -ī), One who speaks through his nose.
s.m. (f. -ī), Singing or hissing (as water on becoming warm); milk-warm, lukewarm, tepid; — snuffling; speaking through the nose; — a snuffler, one who speaks through the nose; — tepidity
subs: v.n. To speak with a snuffle; to speak through the nose ( = gungunānā).

r: gungunāhaṭ, gungunāṭ, gungunānā, āhaṭ, āwaṭ, garmāhaṭ
def: s.f. = gungun, q.v.

ety: gunguna˚ = gungun, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. āwe = S. āpi, caus. aug.
def: v.n. To buzz, to hum; to sing softly or to oneself; to speak or read in a suppressed or inarticulate tone; to mumble, murmur, mutter, grumble; to snuffle, to speak through the nose; — to simmer, to be or become lukewarm or tepid.
v.n. To speak through the nose; to mutter, grumble; to buzz, hum.

ety: gungunā, q.v.+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं
def: s.m. Lukewarmness, tepidity.

r: gungunāṭ, gungunāhaṭ, gungunānā, āhaṭ, āwaṭ, garmāhaṭ
def: s.f. = gungun, q.v.
s.f. = guhāṅjanī, q.v.

adj. & s.m. Endowed with good qualities, or merits, or parts; possessed of art or skill; virtuous, excellent, meritorious, worthy; accomplished, skilful, dexterous; — an excellent or a virtuous man, a perfect man, a saint; an accomplished or a skilful person; — a magician, conjurer, sorcerer (syn. ʻāmil); a charmer, snake-charmer, snake-catcher.
adj. & s.m. = guṇī, q.v.
a corr. old H. form of unīs, q.v.
s.m. A mason's (or a carpenter's) square.
s.m. corr. of گنيه gunya, q.v.
s.m. A Braj pl. of guṇī.
s.f. Humming, buzzing; hum, buzz; resounding; hollow sound; reverberation, echo; roar.
s.f. = gūṅjh, q.v.
(prob. the P. g̠anj arabicized), s.f.(?) Amorous gesture or behaviour, &c. (see g̠amza).

s.f. An echo, a hollow sound; a low murmuring sound, humming, buzzing; — gunj, s.m. (and gunjā, s.f.), The shrub Abrus precatorius, and its seed (which forms the smallest of a jeweller's weights; — syn. gūṅć; and ghuṅćī)
subs: s.f. A necklace, or a rosary, of gunj-seeds

def: s.f. An echo, a hollow sound; a low murmuring sound, humming, buzzing; — gunj, s.m. (and gunjā, s.f.), The shrub Abrus precatorius, and its seed (which forms the smallest of a jeweller's weights; — syn. gūṅć; and ghuṅćī)
subs: s.f. A necklace, or a rosary, of gunj-seeds

ety: fr. gunjā, part. of gunjīdan, denom. v. fr P. gunj, 'space, room, capacity'
def: s.f. The power of containing; capacity, room ( = gunjāʼish).
adj. Capable; — profitable, lucrative.
adj. Thick, dense, close; compact.
s.f. Thickness, denseness, closeness, &c.
v.n. To roar; to growl (cf. gūṅjnā).
adj. Buzzing, humming; making a low murmuring sound.

ety: abst. s. fr. gunjā; see gunjāʼī
def: s.f. Holding, containing; room, capacity; room to contain, stowage; — profit; — the revenue capabilities of a village (esp. with reference to a proposed increase of revenue)
subs: adj. Capable of being held or contained
(-kī), To have room (for), to be capable of holding or containing

def: s.f. (dialec.) Fastening (of a bracelet, &c.), a clasp.
s.f. = gūṅj, 'hum,' &c.

ety: prob. Prk. गुज्झओ; S. गुह्य+कः, rt. गुह्
def: adj. (f. -ī), Grave, serious; venerable.
s.m. = gūjhā, q.v.
s.m. See s.v. gunj.
v.n. = gūṅjnā, q.v.
s.f. (dialec.) The shrub Limonia pentaphylla.
s.f. A sparrow.
s.f. An ear-ring.
v.n. = gūṅjnā, q.v.
v.n. To hum, buzz; to sound (as a drum, &c.); to resound; to reverberate, to echo; to roar; to growl.

ety: for A. غنة, v.n. fr. غنّ 'to speak through the nose'
def: s.m. A nasal sound or twang; the nasal n; — buzzing, humming (of flies, &c.); — adj. Nasal (the letter n); quiescent.
v.t. To praise, laud; — to think upon, consider, judge of; — to put in practice, to practise; — (in Arith.) to multiply (by, -se).
s.f. (rustic) A light whip or scourge (koṛā) of twisted cloth (with which rustic women lash one another at the Holī festival).
s.m. Beam (of a ship); cross-timbers (of a boat; — syn. guṛhā or gūṛhā).
s.m. (Dakh.) Spring; fountain; pool; tank, pit.
s.m. (local), Lands assigned rent-free for religious purposes; — a kind of horse bred in Thibet, &c.; a hill-pony.
v.t. prop. guṅthānā, q.v.
v.t. = gūthnā, q.v.
v.t. A dialec. form. of gūṅthnā, or gūthnā, q.v.
v.t. A dialec. form of guṅthwānā, q.v.
v.t. To cause to string, &c.; — to cause to be strung; to have or get plaited, &c. (see gūṅthnā).
adj. (old H.) = untīs, q.v.
adj. & s.m. See s.v. guṇ.
adj. See s.v. guṇ.
part. adj. & s.m. To be multiplied; — that which is to be multiplied (by), the multiplicand
adj. Hidden, secret; dark, obscure; blind
subs: adj. & adv. Secret; quiet; silent; — in secrecy, or in secret, secretly; quietly; — s.f. Secrecy; silence.
wety: Either onomatopoeic and inherited from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀕𑀧𑁆𑀧- (*gappa-), or inherited from Sanskrit गल्प (galpa). Cognate with Classical Persian گَپ (gap), Punjabi ਗੱਪ (gappa) / گپّ (gappa), Bengali গপ (gop), Gujarati ગપ (gap), Nepali गफ (gaph), Odia ଗପ (gapa), Assamese গপ (gop).

def: s.m. An iron mace; — a throne of iron or of wood.
s.m. corr. of gopāl, q.v.
s.f.(?) (colloq.) A boys' game ( = gup-ćup).
s.m. (f.?) A boys' game (in which one puffs out his cheeks, and another strikes them with both hands so as to produce a sound); — a kind of sweetmeat made of curds.
s.f. A cave, cavern; den; vault; — a retreat, hiding-place; a thicket
subs: To settle down in a cave or cell, to lead the life of a hermit.

def: s.m. A tassel; a wreath; a bunch (of flowers, &c.).

part. adj. (f. -ā), & adv. Protected, guarded, preserved; — hidden, concealed, kept secret; secret; withdrawn from sight, invisible; (in Gram.) not expressed, understood; — secretly, privately; — s.m. An appellation (of frequent use) forming the last member of compound proper names of men of the Vaiśya or third caste
subs: s.f. Income from a hidden or secret source (as bribes, &c.)

s.m. A secret gift or present, a hidden donation; a valuable thing given in a concealed manner to Brāhmans or to a deity (as when a person leaves property with a Brāhman and never reclaims it; or presents him with a sealed bag; or drops a present in a sacred pool in which he has bathed at a solar eclipse, leaving the officiating Brāhmans to search for it)
v.n. To remain hidden, lie concealed; to be or continue invisible
s.f. A beating or hurt that leaves no marks; — taunt, gibe, sneer
s.m. A disguise, or dress, &c., used for concealment; — a false or deceitful person; a hypocrite, dissembler; an actor, a mimic (syn. ćhalī, kaptī; bahu-rūpī)
v.n. To be hidden or concealed; to be invisible.

def: s.f. Protecting, protection; concealing, concealment; a means of protection.
s.f. A hidden sword; a sword-stick.
s.m. = گرو guru, q.v.: — gur-bhāʼī = guru-bhāʼī.
s.m. Raw or coarse sugar (the produce of the first inspissation of the juice of the sugar-cane); molasses, treacle
subs: (S. guḍa+āmra), s.m. Mangoes boiled with meal and sugar (resembling mango-fool)
or guṛ-phūl, s.m. The plant Bassia latifolia (see mahuʼā)
Anything to be said or done clandestinely or in secret; a secret or underhand business or work to be going on.

def: s.m. Unripe grapes or dates; — a colour in pigeons.

ety: v.n. fr. غرب 'to go away, pass away.' &c.
def: s.m. A crow; raven; rook; jackdaw; — a kind of (Arab) boat or vessel (vulg. called a 'grab').
s.m. Weeding; digging up; breaking up and pulverizing the ground in a field; — the process of ploughing through a field of bājrā, &c. when the plant is about a foot high.
s.m. pl. (of g̠arīb), Strangers; the poor.
v.t. To dig, dig up; to weed.
s.m.(?) Name of a medicine, Menispermum glabrum or cordifolium (the infusion of which is used as a febrifuge).

ety: gorā, q.v.+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: s.f. Fairness, whiteness, fair complexion; yellowness; pale redness, ruddiness (syn. gorā-pan).
s.f. Growl; snarl.
ex: listen to the roar of the lion!

ety: prob. a dialec. var. of garajnā
def: v.n. To snarl; to growl (syn. ghuraknā).
wety: Adapted from Sanskrit गर्जति (gárjati). Doublet of گَاجْنَا (gājnā). Compare Maithili गरजब (garᵊjab) / 𑒑𑒩𑒖𑒥 (garajaba), Punjabi ਗਰਜਣਾ (garjaṇā) / گَرجݨا (garjṇā), Gujarati ગરજવું (garajvũ), Marathi गरजणे (garajṇe).

r: g̠uraknā, g̠urkānā, g̠urkī, ghurkānā, ghuraknā, ghurkī
def: v.n. = H غرکانا g̠urkānā, v.t. = H غرکي g̠urkī, s.f. (local) for ghurkānā, ghuraknā, and ghurkī respectively, qq.v.
s.m. A preparation of tobacco and treacle (guṛ) for smoking in a ḥuqqa.
s.m. = guṛ-ambā or guṛ-āmbā, q.v.s.v. guṛ.
v.t. To cause to be dug, to have or get (a hole, &c.) dug or excavated; — to cause to dig; — to dig ( = goṛnā).
imperf. part. of g̠urīdan), Growling; roaring, crying out, making a loud or threatening noise; — fierce, rapacious.

ety: for vurāz; Pehl. varāz; Zend varāza; S. वराह
def: s.m. Wild boar, boar, hog.

ety: for orig. گراز gurāz; fr. Zend vi+rāz = S. वि+राज्
def: adj. Pompous, haughty; frowning; bold, brave.

ety: g̠urāz, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Pompousness, haughtiness; frowning; boldness, bravery.
s.m. A cat
subs: A meek cat; (fig.) a wicked rogue.

def: s.m. corr. of غربا gurabā, q.v.

ety: inf. n. of غرب 'to become a stranger or foreigner, to go to a foreign country'
def: s.f. Travelling (to foreign countries), going abroad; emigration; — being far from (one's) home or native country; the state or condition of a stranger, or foreigner, or exile; wretchedness, misery; humility, lowliness
subs: adj. Oppressed with misery or wretchedness, wretched, miserable.

def: s.m. See s.v. گرو guru.
s.f. The creeping-plant Menispermum glabrum.

ety: Pehl. gūrt˚; Zend vareda, fr. vared; S. वर्ध, rt. वर्ध्
def: adj. Strong, brave, valiant, valorous, warlike.

ety: prob. S. गुड+द+कः, rt. दा 'to cut,' &c.
def: s.m. The scraper at a sugar-mill to prevent the sugar from settling at the bottom of the boiler; — a sugar-mill.
s.m. A kidney; the reins; — courage
s.f. corr. of guru-dakshiṇā, q.v.s.v. گرو guru.

ety: rt. of gureḵẖtan = prep. gu = Zend vi = S. वि+reḵẖtan (rt. rez), fr. Zend rt. rić = S. रिच्
def: part. adj. Flying, fleeing, fugitive (used as last member of compounds); — s.f. Flying (from); flight, escape; avoiding, shunning; a diversion, digression; deviation; regression; abstaining (from), abstinence; fasting; aversion, dislike, abhorrence
subs: (-se), To fly (from); to avoid, shun; to evade (e.g. inṣāf-se gurez karnā); — to abstain (from); to be averse (to), &c.
s.f. Time or place of fleeing; the place (or occasion) in which it is proper to avoid or fly (from a person or thing); place of escape; place to fly to, place of refuge, asylum, retreat.

ety: imperf. part. of gureḵẖtan; see gurez
def: part. Taking flight, fleeing, fugitive.

ety: for A. غرفة, v.n. fr. غرف 'to take out'; 'to cut,' &c.
def: s.m. An upper chamber or apartment; a hall, a parlour; — a window.

r: g̠urfish, g̠urwish, g̠urbish, g̠urwīdan, g̠urumbīdan, gared, gar, g̠urrish
ety: prob. for orig. g̠urwish, or g̠urbish, fr. g̠urwīdan = g̠urumbīdan; rt. Zend gared, fr. gar = S. गर्; cf. g̠urrish
def: s.f. Reproof, chiding, threatening, threat, intimidation, bullying (as of a coward who affects bravery), bluster
subs: (-se), To practise or try intimidation or bullying (with or on).

ety: Pehl. gurg or vurg; Zend vehrka; S. वृक
def: s.m. A wolf
subs: s.m. A wolf's whelp; a young wolf.

def: s.m. A scullion, a menial servant, servant boy (syn. bāsan-mājhā; ṭahluʼā); a dependent menial; — a low fellow, a vagabond.
s.m. Knee-joint.
s.m. = گرگا gurgā, q.v
s.f. A kind of shoe or slipper.
s.m. (dialec.) The knee-joint ( = gurgā, q.v.).
s.f. A dialec. form of the next, q.v.
s.f. Knee-breeches, short drawers (cf. guṛgī).
s.f. = گرگي gurgī, q.v.
s.f. A menial servant; a wench; a slave-girl.

ety: by redupl. of g̠ur = Zend g̠ar = S. गर् (गॄ)
def: adj. Growling; grumbling; muttering with rage; — ruptured; — s.f. Growling; raging; noise, uproar, tumult.
s.f. The cold fit of a fever, ague, quotidian fever (cf. guṛguṛī).
s.f. A small ḥuqqa, a kind of pipe for smoking.
s.m. Beam, cross-timber (of a ship or boat).
s.m. = guṛhā, and gūṅṛā, qq.v.
part. Covered, veiled, hidden, concealed; secret, private; disguised; — difficult, abstruse, occult, obscure; abstract; mysterious, enigmatical
subs: (˚ha+ar˚), Of obscure or hidden meaning; of abstruse import
or gūṛh-vaktā, s.m. (lit. 'saying profound things'), A pedagogue; a pedant
s.m. A secret emissary; a disguised agent; a spy
vulg. gūṛhaj, adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Born privately or secretly; — the son of a concealed birth; a son born secretly of a woman whose husband is absent, the real father being unknown
s.m. A secret emissary; a spy
s.m. A secret way; a subterraneous passage; a bye-road

def: s.m. 'The shoe-flower,' the plant Hibiscus syriacus.

ety: gūṛh(nā) + al = il = Prk. इदो = S. इतः
def: part. adj. (dialec.) Well-threshed, well-beaten.
s.f. = H گوڙهتو गूढ़त्व gūṛhatva, s.m. (S. gūḍhatā; gūḍhatva), Concealedness; concealment; secrecy.
s.m. = H گوڙهتا गूढ़ता gūṛhatā, s.f. (S. gūḍhatā; gūḍhatva), Concealedness; concealment; secrecy.
qq.v.

ety: prob. govar or gobar, q.v.+S. प्रकरः
def: s.m. A heap or stack of cow-dung.
s.f. A glass bead; bead or stone (of a rosary, or necklace, &c.); — vertebra, a joint (of the spine).

ety: guṛ, q.v. + iyā = Prk. इओ = S. इकः
def: s.m. A seller of guṛ or raw sugar.
s.f. = guṛiyā, q.v.
s.m. = گريا guriyā, q.v.
s.f. A doll, a puppet; — (fig.) a humble or poor bride
subs: s.m. A doll's marriage; — a poor man's wedding
s.m. Playing with dolls; — an easy matter.

def: s.m. The country of Guzerat (in western India); — the people of Guzerat; — a native of Guzerat.
s.f. Name of a Rāgiṇī or musical mode.
s.m. A Georgian; — a servant-boy; — a hut; — a kind of dog.

r: g̠urkānā, g̠uraknā, g̠urkī, ghurkānā, ghuraknā, ghurkī
def: v.t. = H غرکنا g̠uraknā, v.n. = H غرکي g̠urkī, s.f. (local) for ghurkānā, ghuraknā, and ghurkī respectively, qq.v.
s.f. A kind of mortgage (in which the mortgager has to pay three-fourths of the revenue of the mortgaged land).

r: g̠urkī, g̠urkānā, g̠uraknā, ghurkānā, ghuraknā, ghurkī
def: s.f. = H غرکانا g̠urkānā, v.t. = H غرکنا g̠uraknā, v.n. (local) for ghurkānā, ghuraknā, and ghurkī respectively, qq.v.
v.t. (dialec.) To tread out (corn); to thresh, &c.

ety: Pārsī kirūh or kiroh?; Zend kharedha; cf. S. वर्ग
def: s.m.(f.?) A collection or party (of men), company, band, troop, crew, gang, group; people, levy of people; a class, an order.
s.m. = غره g̠urra, q.v.

ety: for A. غرة g̠urrat, inf. n. of غرّ 'to have whiteness, or a white patch, &c. on the forehead' (a horse
def: s.m. Whiteness; clearnness, brightness; — a white mark, a star, or blaze on the forehead of a horse; — a speckled, or iron-grey, or roan horse; — the first, or commencement (of the month, &c.), the night in which the new moon is seen, the first day of the moon, calends; — the first appearance or dawn of day, morning or its splendour; — the face, aspect, forehead, front (of a man); — the first, the best, or the chiefest (of anything); — g̠urra batānā, or g̠urra denā (-ko), To put (one) off to the (next) new moon; to put off, to shuffle with, to get rid of, &c.; to pass over or by (syn. ṭāl-denā).

ety: g̠ur˚, prob. = S. गर् (गॄ); but cf. S. घुर् cf. also P. g̠urrīdan, whence g̠urrish: — rt. Zend g̠ar = S. गर्
def: v.n. To growl; to roar; to purr (as a cat); to frown, look menacing.
s.f. = غره g̠urra, q.v.
s.f. Parched barley.

ety: abst. s. fr. g̠urrīdan, g̠urīdan; rt. Zend gar = S. गर् (गॄ)
def: s.f. Growling; roaring; fury, rage, anger, indignation
subs: v.n. To growl; to roar; to rage, &c.

def: s.f. An earthen vessel or bowl (such as a goldsmith uses for his fire); fire-pan, brasier; fire-place, grate (cf. bursī); — (dialec.) cot, cottage, cabin, hut.
s.f. = گڙسي gursī, q.v.

adj. & s.m. (fem. gurvi, or gurviṇī), Heavy, weighty; great, large, extended, long (as a vowel), grave (as an accent); difficult, hard; high (in degree), vehement, violent, excessive, deep, much; important, serious, momentous; valuable, highly prized, dear, beloved; venerable, honourable, respectable; excellent; best; — one who is an object of reverence, a venerable or respectable person (as a father, mother, &c.); a spiritual parent or preceptor (from whom a youth receives the initiatory Mantra or prayer and who conducts the necessary ceremonies up to the period of investiture with the sacrificial thread); a spiritual guide, a religious teacher (one who explains the law and religion to his pupil, instructs him in the Śāstras, &c.); a pastor; a teacher (generally); a superior; — a name of Brihaspati or the planet Jupiter (who is considered as the guru or preceptor of the gods); — a wise man, a sage ( = gur-gyānī); — (ironic.) a consummate knave
subs: vulg. gur-bhāʼī, s.m. Follower of the same spiritual preceptor or guide, fellow-sectarian, fellow-disciple; fellow-student
s.m. (f. -ī), The child (natural or adopted) of a religious preceptor

vulg. gur-jan, s.m. pl. Religious or venerable persons, eminent persons, great people, respectable people; — s.m. sing. A venerable person (as a father, a religious preceptor, &c.), an elder, a superior
vulg. gur-daććhanā, s.f. A fee given to a spiritual preceptor; rent-free land given to a religious teacher
s.m. The pronouncing a blessing on a religious guide
s.m. A respectful epithet of any guru
s.m. The eighth Nakshatra or lunar constellation Pushya (of which Brihaspati is the ruling deity)
s.m. A serious or momentous affair; — the business or office of a spiritual teacher
s.m. 'A guru with bells' (hung round his neck), a thorough knave
vulg. guru-laćh, or gur-laćh, s.m. = next
adj. (f. -ā), Heavy, hard of digestion; — s.m. Knowledge which can be acquired from a living instructor only
s.f. The wife of a spiritual teacher
(or gur-) mukh honā, v.n. To receive the initiatory Mantra from a guru; to become a disciple or pupil
s.f. Name of the character in which the Panjābī language is written
vulg. gur-bār, s.m. 'The day of Jupiter,' Thursday.

def: s.m. = guru-laksh, q.v.s.v. guru.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit गौरव (gaurava, “cumbrousness, importance”); see गौर (gaura, “shining, brilliant”) for more.

def: s.f. The office, or profession, or condition of a guru or spiritual preceptor.
s.f. The wife of a spiritual preceptor; a religious preceptress.
s.f. = guruʼāʼin, q.v.

ety: inf. n. of غرب 'to be hidden'; 'to set,' &c.
def: s.m. Setting (of the sun, &c.); occultation; — sunset; — the west
subs: v.n. To set (the sun, or moon, &c.).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian غروب (ġurūb), from Arabic غُرُوب (ḡurūb).

def: s.m. (orig.) 'A thing by which one is deceived'; pride, haughtiness, vanity, vainglory
subs: v.n. To practise haughtiness, to be proud, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Persian غرور, from Arabic غُرُور (ḡurūr).

def: s.f. = g̠urūr.
v.n. (dialec.) To cry, stopping the breath; to sob.
adj. Hungry.
s.f. Hunger.
s.f. Weight, heaviness; burden, trouble; weightiness, gravity, importance, dignity; — the position or office of a (religious) teacher, tutorship.
adj. See s.v. گرو guru.
s.m. = gurutā, q.v.
adj. & s.f. Heavy (with child), pregnant; — a pregnant woman; — the wife of a guru or teacher.
adj. & s.f. Heavy (with child), pregnant; — a pregnant woman ( = garbhinī).

ety: Pehl. vazr; Zend vazra, fr. vaz, 'To strengthen,' &c.; S. वज्र, rt. वज्
def: s.m. A mace, a club; an iron club (pointed at one end, and having a knob at the other covered with spikes); — a battle-axe
subs: s.m. A (Mohammadan) faqīr who carries a club armed with spikes with which he wounds himself to extort alms.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گُرْز (gurz). In the Indo-Persian tradition, it became associated with lightning-bolts, thunder and storms. It became a metaphor for strength and force as well, especially in relation to the warrior and fighter.

def: s.m. A bound, jump.

s.m. 'The master or possessor of cows or of herds'; an epithet of the Deity; — a religious mendicant (a faqīr), a devotee, a saint or holy man; — a caste of Brāhmaṇs, descendants of the disciples of Ćaitanya of Nadiyā; a Brāhmaṇ of this class; — an honorary title affixed to proper names.

ety: g̠uṣṣa, q.v. + Prk. इल्लो and इल्लओ = S. इलः and इल+कः
def: adj. Angry, wrathful, passionate, irascible, choleric, furious, fierce.

s.m. 'The master or possessor of cows or of herds'; an epithet of the Deity; — a religious mendicant (a faqīr), a devotee, a saint or holy man; — a caste of Brāhmaṇs, descendants of the disciples of Ćaitanya of Nadiyā; a Brāhmaṇ of this class; — an honorary title affixed to proper names.
s.m. = gusāʼīṅ, q.v.

ety: for A. غسالة, v.n. fr. غسل 'to wash'
def: s.m. Water with which a thing has been washed; — bathing.
s.m. = next, q.v.

r: gusār, guzār, guẕār, gusārdan, guzārdan, guẕāshtan
ety: i.q. guzār, or guẕār, q.v.; — rt. of gusārdan = guzārdan = guẕāshtan
def: part. adj. Letting go; taking away, removing, dissipating, assuaging; drinking, swallowing, taking down (used as last member of compounds, e.g. g̠am-gusār, mai-gusār, qq.v.).
adj. (f. -ī), Passionate, irascible, furious, choleric.
s.f. An assembly, a meeting, a cabal, a council; a society, an association
subs: v.n. A council to be held, &c.

def: s.f. An assembly, a meeting, a cabal, a council; a society, an association
subs: v.n. A council to be held, &c.

ety: rt. of gusistan; — ˚sil, for sal = Zend śar = S. शर्; see gusista
def: part. adj. Breaking, tearing; destroying; removing (used as last member of compounds; e.g. gam-gusil, 'destroying or removing care').

ety: g̠uṣṣa, q.v. + Prk. इल्लो and इल्लओ = S. इलः and इल+कः
def: adj. Angry, wrathful, passionate, irascible, choleric, furious, fierce.

r: gusista, gusistan, gusīḵẖtan, gu, vi, sistan, sīḵẖtan, śar
ety: perf. part. of gusistan = gusīḵẖtan; gu = Zend vi = S. वि; sistan or sīḵẖtan, fr. Zend rt. śar = S. शर् (श्टनाति
def: part. adj. Broken; interrupted; abrupt.
s.f. Breaking; breach, fracture; interruption.
s.m. A complete (purificatory) washing of the whole person; bathing, ablution, a bath
subs: (-ko), To wash, bathe; to wash a corpse ( = g̠usl-ě-maiyit denā)
or g̠usl-ě-siḥḥat, s.m. Bathing after recovery from sickness
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian غسل (ġusl), from Arabic غُسْل (ḡusl).

ety: for A. غصة, v.n. fr. غصّ 'to be choked,' &c.
def: s.m. Choking, strangulation, suffocation; — (choking) wrath, rage, anger, passion; — grief, disquietude of mind, anxiety; — g̠uṣṣe, adv. In anger; from or through anger
subs: or g̠uṣṣa thūknā or thūk-denā, or g̠uṣṣa khānā or khā-jānā, or g̠uṣṣa mārnā (apnā), 'To gulp down, or spit out, or swallow, (one's) anger,' to suppress anger, restrain or subdue wrath
(-ko), or g̠uṣṣe-meṅ lānā (-ko), To make angry, to enrage, put into a state of anger or wrath, to provoke, to offend
(or g̠uṣṣe-ke māre) bhūt ho-jānā, To become a very devil through rage, to rage like a fiend
(-par), To be angry or in a passion (with), to rage (against)
To be filled with anger or rage, to be or become enraged
v.n. To be easily offended, to be very choleric, or irritable
adj. Angry, wrathful, passionate, choleric, furious, fierce

def: s.m. corr. of غصه guṣṣa, q.v.
adj. Presumptuous, arrogant, insolent, audacious, impudent, saucy, uncivil, rude; cruel; abrupt.
adv. Presumptuously, arrogantly, rudely, &c. (see gustāḵẖ).
s.f. Presumption, arrogance, insolence, audacity, assurance, sauciness, rudeness; contempt (of court); cruelty
subs: To presume, to be arrogant, or insolent, or rude, &c.

ety: rt. of gustardan; — gu = Zend vi = S. वि; ˚stardan, fr. Zend śtar = S. स्तर् (स्तृ)
def: part. adj. & s.m. Spreading, strewing, scattering, diffusing; — spreader, &c.; dispenser; administrator (used as last member of compounds, e.g. ʻadālat-gustar, 'One who diffuses or dispenses justice').

ety: perf. part. of gustardan; see gustar
def: part. Spread (as a bed, &c.); having spread; — s.m. That which is spread; a foundation.

ety: gustar, q.v.+ī = Zend i = S. इ
def: s.f. Spreading, scattering, strewing; diffusing; dispensing, &c. (used as last member of compounds; e.g. ʻadālat-gustarī, 'the dispensing or administering of justice').
v.t. To swallow, gulp, bolt; — v.n. To coo (as a dove, &c.)
v.n. To coo.
v.t. To thread, string; to entangle; to entwine; to embarrass; to engage in; — to fix; to contrive.
s.m. = غٿرغون g̠uṭar-g̠ūṅ, q.v.
s.m. A kind of prickly plant, Solanum melongena (syn. bhaṭ-kaṭaiyā).

ety: Prk. गुत्थं; S. ग्रथितं; or v.n. of gūthnā, q.v.
def: s.m. A round ball (of cotton, &c.); a string or thread (of anything).
part. adj. (f. -ī), Blunted; set on edge (the teeth).
s.m. A large lump; a large stone (of fruit), &c. ( = guṭṭhal, and guṭhlī).
v.n. To be dulled or blunted; to be set on edge (the teeth).

s.f. A knot; a lump, mass (as of curd in milk, or of flour in broth, &c.), a clot (of blood); a ball, lump, gathering (as of cotton in a quilt, &c.); — kernel; stone (of a fruit); — a gland; a glandular swelling; a bump; a hard boil or tumour; hard lump (in a woman's breast); hard part (of a boil, &c.); a lump of hardened fæces.

ety: gūth˚ = Prk. गुत्थो = S. ग्रथितः, rt. ग्रथ्
def: v.t. To thread, string; to plait, braid; to stitch; to put on a spit or skewer, to spit ( = gūṅthnā).

ety: gut˚ or guth˚ = Prk. गुत्थ(इ) = S. ग्रथ्य(ते), pass. of rt. ग्रथ्
def: v.n. To be strung, to be threaded; to be interlaced, or intertwined, or intermingled; to be braided, be plaited; to be or become tangled (as thread, &c.), to be ravelled; to tangle; to catch, hitch; to be entangled (in); to be embarrassed, be involved in difficulties; — to close (with, -se, an antagonist, in wrestling, syn. juṭnā); — to be taken up (with), be occupied, or engaged (in), be absorbed (in); to be under employment, or in use
subs: (dialec.), v.t. To settle, fix, determine, contrive ( = gutānā).

ety: Prk. गुत्थओ (with w inserted); S. ग्रथित+कः
def: adj. (f. -wīṅ), Strung together; interlaced, intertwined; plaited, &c.
v.t. = guṅthwānā, q.v.
s.f. A pill, bolus; a small globe or ball, a bullet, a marble, a pellet; a pearl.
s.m. (dialec.) = guttā, q.v.

s.m. A small ball, a bolus; a magic ball prepared by devotees (which, if put into the mouth, is supposed to make a person invisible, or to accomplish something wonderful); — a sweetmeat swallowed as a bolus without chewing; — a small book worn as an amulet; — a little book (bound or boarded); a handbook, a manual; — a flake of pān or betel-leaf (syn. khīlī); a mouthful; a gulp; — a round pebble, a shard (used in play; cf. giṭkarī, and gittī).

ety: guṭkā or giṭkā, q.v. + S. इनी
def: adj. & s.f. = guṭṭī, fem. of guṭṭā, q.v.
s.m. = H گٿلي गुटली guṭlī, s.f. dialec. forms of guṭhlā, and guṭhlī, qq.v.
s.f. = H گٿلا गुटला guṭlā, s.m. dialec. forms of guṭhlā, and guṭhlī, qq.v.

ety: gut˚ or guth˚ = Prk. गुत्थ(इ) = S. ग्रथ्य(ते), pass. of rt. ग्रथ्
def: v.n. To be strung, to be threaded; to be interlaced, or intertwined, or intermingled; to be braided, be plaited; to be or become tangled (as thread, &c.), to be ravelled; to tangle; to catch, hitch; to be entangled (in); to be embarrassed, be involved in difficulties; — to close (with, -se, an antagonist, in wrestling, syn. juṭnā); — to be taken up (with), be occupied, or engaged (in), be absorbed (in); to be under employment, or in use
subs: (dialec.), v.t. To settle, fix, determine, contrive ( = gutānā).

def: s.m. (dialec.) Exclusive right (of sale, supply, &c.); monopoly, contract; — an income of variable amount sold (or let) for a fixed sum; lease (of land, &c.), farm (syn. ijāra).
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Short of stature, stumpy, stunted; — a stumpy person, a dwarf.

ety: prob. Prk. गुत्थलो or गुट्ठलो(?); S. ग्रथ्+अलः
def: adj. & s.m. Having knots, knotted; lumpy, bumpy; coagulated; hardened; having a large stone (as a mango, &c.); — a knot; a hard lump; a large bump; coagulation; — a gland; a glandular swelling; a hard boil or tumour (cf. guṭhlī).
s.f. Betel-nut, the nut of Areca catechu.
s.m. A witness, an evidence; testimony, proof
subs: or gawāh ṭhaharānā (-ko), To make (one) a witness, to name (a person) as a witness
or gawāh karnā (-ko), To bring forward (or to produce) evidence or a witness or witnesses; to make (one) a witness
(-ko), To bring evidence; to take a witness; to invoke
(-par), To become a witness; to bear testimony (to).

def: s.f. Evidence, witness, testimony; deposition; written testimony
subs: (-par), To give evidence, to witness (to), bear testimony (to), testify, attest; to depose (to)
or gawāhī likhnā (-par), To witness (a document), to attest.

ety: P. go+waiyā, as in the preceding
def: adj. Conversible; eloquent; — s.f. A gossip; a (woman's) female friend, &c. ( = guʼiyāṅ, q.v.).
s.m. The betel-nut tree, Areca faufel or catechu.

ety: rt. of guwāshtan, or guwārīdan; gu = Zend vi = S. वि+wār, fr. Pehl. var = Zend var = S. वर् (वृ)
def: part. adj. Digestible; palatable, &c. (see guwārā; — it is used as last member of compounds, e.g. ḵẖẉush-gawār, and nā-gawār, q.v.s.v. ḵẖẉush and nā).
adj. = gawārā, q.v.

ety: imperf. part. of guwārīdan; see guwār
def: part. adj. Digesting; stomaching, swallowing, putting up with; — digestible; palatable; agreeable, pleasant
subs: v.t. To make (a thing) palatable (to oneself, apne-ūpar); to take (upon oneself); to put up with, to submit to, to stomach, to swallow, to endure or bear.

def: s.f. An electuary (or a compound) for assisting digestion.
s.m. A fart
subs: (-ko), 'To regard as a camel's fart'; to disregard, to treat lightly.

ety: prob. akin to S. गोदः or गोर्दः; H. gūdā
def: s.m. A nut; a walnut; a kernel (cf. ćil-goza).
s.f. An inconsiderate word, a vain or rash speech; boasting; vanity; — a falsehood, a lie

ety: imperf. part. of guzīdan = prep. gu+zīdan = ćīdan; Zend vi+rt. ći; S. वि+चि (चिनोति)
def: part. adj. Choosing, selecting; adopting (see guzīn).

ety: rt. of guẕārdan or guẕāshtan, causat. of guẕashtan; gu˚ = Zend vi = S. वि; — zash˚, fr. Zend rt. tar = S. तर्(तॄ)
def: s.m. A passing; passage, transit; a ferry (cf. guẕāra, and guẕar).
(For this and other words beginning with guz˚ which are not to be found below, see under گذ guẓ˚.)
s.m. A passing by; ingress and egress; a passage; a path, way, road; a pass; a ford; a ferry; a toll-bar; — escape; — jurisdiction; — living, life, &c. ( = guẕāra, guẕrān); — adj. Passing, elapsing
subs: s.m. A ford; a canal; an aqueduct
s.m. A ferry-man; an officer who collects the customs at ferries; a toll-collector; — a guardian at a pass; a road-watch
s.m., or guẕar-gāh, or guẕar-gāh-ě-ʻām, s.f. A passage; a ferry; a ford; a public road, highway, thoroughfare
v.n. To pass; to pass time, &c. ( = guẕrān karnā, q.v.)

def: s.m. = گذاره guẕāra, q.v.
s.m. (see guẕār), s.m. A passing, passing over, crossing; a passage; passing of time or of life; living, subsisting; — stay, abode; — a ferry; ferry-boat; a toll-bar
subs: To effect a passage, to cross over; to get through or across; — to live, to subsist (on, -se); to drag out (one's) days (by means of, -se), to subsist with difficulty
To hit on (or to discover) a means of living

r: guẕarān, guẕrān, guẕashtan, guẕar, āna, ana
ety: imperf. part. of guẕashtan; and = guẕar, q.v.+Zend āna or ana = S. अन]
def: part. Passing; passing by; passing beyond; — (v.n. of guẕarānīdan), s.f. Passing; passing time or life, living; life; course of life; subsistence; livelihood, employment
subs: To pass or spend time, to pass life, to live, to subsist; to contrive to live, to drag out (one's) days.

ety: perf. part. of guẕar-jānā = guẕarnā, q.v.
def: part. adj. (fem. guẕar-gaʼī), Passed away, lapsed; deceased, dead.

ety: guẕār, q.v.+ī = Zend i = S. इ
def: s.f. Passing; returning; payment, &c. (see guẕār, like which it is used in comp., e.g. shukr-guẕārī, q.v.).
s.f. A market held (in the afternoon) by the road-side or in a thoroughfare
subs: To set up a stall, or lay out things for sale, in a road-side market.

def: s.f. Payment; tribute; representation, explanation; statement; petition, request
subs: (kī ḵẖidmat-meṅ), To represent (to); to state (syn. ʻarẓ karnā).

def: v.t. To cause to pass; to bring to pass; to pass (life, or time, &c.); to pass by; — to pass or lay before; to exhibit; to state.
wety: From Classical Persian گذار (guzār) + ـنا (-nā, verbal suffix).

def: v.n. To pass, go, elapse; to come to pass, to happen, to befall; to pass (by or over, -par); to pass (through, par-se, or se); to pass (before, or under, or in review, -se), to be put or laid (before, -se), be presented; to pass (over, -se), to overlook, to omit; to abstain (from), desist (from); to decline; — to pass (beyond), to surpass; to pass away, to die
subs: v.n. (intens.), To pass by, to pass away, to elapse; to die; to move away (from, -se); to pass beyond, to surpass, to outdo
or dar-guẕar karnā (-se), To pass (over), to overlook, to excuse; — to abstain (from), desist (from), to give up; to avoid.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گذر (guzar) + ـنا (-nā, verbal suffix).

ety: v.n. of guẕāshtan, caus. of guẕashtan; see guẕār
def: s.f. The quitting or leaving; — a deduction (from); — a grant (of land).

ety: perf. part. of guẕashtan; see guẕār
def: part. adj. Past, elapsed; last (year, &c.); left; tendered; — s.m. The past.

ety: perf. part. of guzīdan; see guzān
def: part. Chosen, selected; preferred; — choice, select; excellent.
adj. To be chosen, worthy of being chosen.
part. adj. Choosing, selecting; electing; preferring; adopting (used as last member of compounds)
subs: adj. & s.m. Making choice of seclusion, fond of retirement or solitude; solitary; retiring from the world; — a recluse, hermit.

def: s.m. (?) Aid, help, remedy (syn. ćāra); — escape
part. adj. (f. -ī), Passed; passed away, lapsed.
v.t. To pass away time, &c. ( = guẕārnā); to pass before, to put or lay before, to present, offer; to bring forward, to adduce, to produce.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گذران (guzarān) + ـنا (-nā, verbal suffix).

def: s.m. A cartilage; gristle.

r: gwāl, guʼāl, goʼāl, gwālā, guʼālā, goʼālā
def: s.m. A caste of Hindūs whose occupation it is to attend to cattle; a man of that caste; a cow-keeper, cow-herd; a milkman
subs: or gwālā-bāl, s.m. A cow-herd's child or children

r: gwālā, guʼālā, goʼālā, gwāl, guʼāl, goʼāl
def: s.m. A caste of Hindūs whose occupation it is to attend to cattle; a man of that caste; a cow-keeper, cow-herd; a milkman
subs: or gwālā-bāl, s.m. A cow-herd's child or children

def: s.f. A woman of the gwāl caste; the wife of a gwālā; a cowherdess; a milk-maid.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Cow-keeping; — a cow-keeper, cow-herd, &c. ( = gwāl).
s.f. = gwālan, q.v.
s.f. A woman of the gwāl caste; the wife of a gwālā; a cowherdess; a milk-maid.
s.m. = H گيابهن ग्याभन gyābhan, adj. = gābh, and gābhin, qq.v.
adj. = H گيابهہ ग्याभ gyābh, s.m. = gābh, and gābhin, qq.v.
s.m. Knowing, knowledge, understanding, intelligence, apprehension, conception, intellect; — knowledge of a specific and religious kind, which tends to exempt the soul from further transmigration
subs: (apnā), To exercise the understanding; to meditate deeply
s.f. 'The ragged covering of wisdom or of a sage'; Friar's rags
or gyān-wanṭ, or gyān-wantā, adj. & s.m. Knowing, wise, intelligent, judicious (syn. gyānī); — a wise man, a sage, &c.
(˚na+ind˚), s.m. An organ of perception or of consciousness (e.g. the skin, tongue, eye, ear, mental powers, intellect).

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Knowing, wise, learned, intelligent, judicious, discreet; — one who understands fully; one who possesses gyān or religious wisdom; — a sage; a philosopher; — a prognosticator; an astrologer; a soothsayer; a fortune-teller.

adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Knowing, wise, learned, intelligent, judicious, discreet; — one who understands fully; one who possesses gyān or religious wisdom; — a sage; a philosopher; — a prognosticator; an astrologer; a soothsayer; a fortune-teller.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Making known; giving information; proclaiming; — one who makes known or who proclaims; a teacher; a herald.
s.m. Making known, notifying, informing, apprizing, communicating: gyāpanārth (˚na+ar˚), adv. For the purpose of apprizing, &c.
part. (f. -ā), Made known; notified; revealed; discovered.
part. Taught; expounded; made known.
s.f. Knowing, understanding, comprehension, the exercise of the intellectual faculty.
adj. Eleven ( = igārah, q.v.).

ety: g̠yārah, q.v. + Prk. अवँओ = अमओ = S. अ+म+कः
def: adj. (f. -wīṅ), Eleventh.
adj. a dialec. form of next, q.v.
adj. = gyārahwāṅ, q.v.
part. Known, comprehended, understood
subs: s.m. One well versed in any science or śāstra.

def: adj. & s.m. Acquainted (with), knowing, intelligent, wise; — one who knows, &c.
subs: (˚tā+an˚), s.m. A name of Varddhamān, the last Jin or Jain Pontiff.

def: s.m. Relationship, affinity.
s.m. A father; a kinsman; a distant kinsman who does not participate in the oblations of food and water offered to deceased ancestors.
adj. & s.m. Acquainted (with), knowing, intelligent, wise; — one who knows, &c.
subs: (˚tā+an˚), s.m. A name of Varddhamān, the last Jin or Jain Pontiff.

def: intj. Ah! alas; oh!; — fie! shame!; — well! excellent! just so! very well! yes indeed (cf. hāṅ)
subs: or hā-hā khānā, or hā-ṛe khānā, v.n. To beg humbly, to implore, entreat.

r: hā, hāʼe-hawwaz, h, hawwaz, hāʼe-mudawwara, ha, h., hāʼe-jalī, hāʼe-z̤āhir, hāʼe-malfūz̤ī, bādshāh, hāʼe-mukhtafī, hāʼe-maktūbī, bahāna, ha-kār
ety: called hāʼe-hawwaz, 'h of the word hawwaz,' or hāʼe-mudawwara, 'the round h'
def: s.m. The letter or sound ha.
s.m. A bubble
subs: v.n. Bubbles to rise, to bubble
adj. (f. -ī), 'Bubble-like,' very frail, or thin, or slight, or little.

def: adj. Of or resembling a bubble; full of bubbles; (in comp.) bubbling.
s.m. (dialec.) = habbā-ḍabbā, q.v.
adv. Voraciously (i.q. hapak-kar).
v.t. &c. = hapaknā, q.v.
